ops.title: iOS focus dot appearance is a pure derivation mirroring web
ops.body: `dotAppearance(_ status: StepStatus?) -> DotAppearance` in `ios/CcPresent/Screens/FocusDeckView.swift` is the single source of the focus-rail glyph mapping (size, fill, stroke), extracted so cross-client styling stays testable: nil → 4pt `borderStrong` tick, `.undecided` → 9pt hollow `warn` stroke, `.approved`/`.rejected`/`.decided` → 9pt filled approve/reject/accentInk. `FocusProgressDotTests.swift` drives it through the real `focusSteps` → `stepStatus` pipeline, mirroring `web/src/components/FocusProgress.test.tsx` case for case. When web's dot vocabulary changes (`FocusProgress.tsx` / `focus.css` `.focus-dot`), change the mapping HERE and its table together — not inline in `dot(_:position:)`, which only draws what the derivation says. Parity is behavioral, not pixel: iOS keeps circles and `borderStrong` edging where web uses rounded squares with status-matched borders, deliberately. VoiceOver reads untallied steps as "no decision" (`dotLabel`). Design rationale and the one-rail-per-screen rule: see the web-side note (untallied steps are ticks; one tally rail per screen) — do not re-add a per-item tally rail to focus mode on either client. Landed as 771aaeeb (cc-task 95075c09).
